name: Internet Trust Board Narrative Add-On
controlling_principle: CyberShield does not ask whether we trust a person. It asks which claims, artifacts, evidence paths, and reliance decisions can be trusted, at what confidence, for what purpose, and with what risk if wrong.
boundary: Static advisory prototype narrative only. No live extraction, live verification, live evidence retrieval, live URL ingestion, live scoring, political validation, legal determination, report delivery, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation is performed.
purpose: Convert the V59.3 Internet Trust Proof Pack export contract into concise board and executive language for artifact reliance decisions.

narrative.executive_summary: CyberShield reviewed a prototype internet artifact through the Internet Trust workflow and mapped its claims to model traces, evidence states, reliance actions, and proof-ready outputs. The artifact is useful for demonstration and education, but most factual claims require verification before executive, public, policy, legal, financial, or operational reliance.
narrative.decision_required: Leadership must decide whether to use the artifact only as a demonstration, constrain reliance until evidence is verified, or assign a human reviewer to validate claims before external use.
narrative.why_it_matters: Internet artifacts can look credible, polished, and data-driven while still containing claims that depend on time period, jurisdiction, source methodology, definitions, legal rules, or changing policy context. Acting on unverified claims can create reputational, operational, legal, financial, or governance risk.
narrative.what_reviewed: CyberShield reviewed static artifact metadata, sample claim rows, claim types, evidence states, model traces, runtime reliance actions, human review requirements, and Proof Pack export sections.
narrative.what_unverified: The prototype does not verify URLs, retrieve sources, validate fiscal numbers, confirm current law or policy, or prove claim accuracy. Claims marked needs_verification require primary-source review before high-stakes reliance.
narrative.reliance_risk: Use for internal demonstration and governance design only unless a qualified human validates claim evidence. Public, board, customer, legal, financial, or policy use requires evidence verification and explicit limitations.
narrative.recommended_next_move: Assign a human reviewer, verify the claims marked needs_verification against authoritative sources, update the evidence state, regenerate the Proof Pack export, and keep limitations attached to any board or public narrative.
narrative.limitations: This is a static advisory prototype. It does not perform live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live claim extraction, live scoring, political validation, legal determination, report delivery, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation.

board_decision_options.label: Use as Demonstration Only
board_decision_options.description: Safe for internal product demonstration and workflow discussion with limitations disclosed.

board_decision_options.label: Constrain Reliance Until Verified
board_decision_options.description: Use the artifact only after claims marked needs_verification are checked against authoritative evidence.

board_decision_options.label: Escalate for Human Review
board_decision_options.description: Assign a qualified reviewer for policy, legal, fiscal, public, or executive reliance.

definition_of_done: Board narrative appears in Proof Pack and Evidence
Narrative pulls from V59.3 export posture and V59.2 claim review counts where available
Narrative modal and text download include explicit limitations
No live extraction, live verification, live scoring, or new top-level tab
